narrative:
irst Warn Forecast – Saturday, May 9, 2026
First Warn Headlines
Areas of dense fog this morning will gradually improve by late morning.
Warm temperatures return this afternoon with highs climbing into the upper 70s to near 80°.
A cold front will bring scattered showers tonight, mainly north of I-70, with a brief rumble of thunder possible.
Cooler air settles in Sunday and Monday with highs dropping back into the 60s.

After a foggy and damp start to the morning across portions of Missouri and Illinois, conditions will steadily improve through the late morning hours as low clouds lift and sunshine begins to break through. Some areas of dense fog may reduce visibility early, especially across central and northeast Missouri, so use caution if you are traveling this morning. Once the fog clears out, we are looking at a very pleasant spring afternoon across the region. Southwest winds and increasing sunshine will help temperatures climb well into the 70s, with a few spots flirting with the 80-degree mark. This will likely be one of the warmest days we have seen so far this month.

Clouds will begin increasing later this afternoon and evening as another cold front approaches from the northwest. Scattered showers are expected to develop this evening and tonight, especially along and north of the I-70 corridor from central Missouri into west-central Illinois. While severe weather is not expected, a few isolated thunderstorms cannot be ruled out. Rainfall coverage will remain scattered, so not everyone will see rain, but keep the umbrella nearby if you have evening plans.

Behind the front, cooler air returns for Sunday and Monday. Temperatures Sunday will depend heavily on how quickly clouds clear out behind the system. Areas that remain cloudy longer may struggle to get out of the low to mid-60s, while locations seeing more sunshine could push closer to 70 degrees.

Looking ahead, dry and seasonably cool weather settles in for the start of the new work week before temperatures rebound again by Tuesday. Another cold front is expected midweek, but forecast confidence remains somewhat lower regarding just how cool temperatures may become behind it. Overall though, the pattern continues to favor periodic rain chances without any major widespread storm systems at this time.
